# UC-10.14.2 ¬∑ ICS Protocol Allow-List Violation

## Description

Detects protocols outside the approved OT baseline (MITRE ATT&CK for ICS T0886), limiting lateral movement and unsafe cross-zone traffic.

## Value

Detects protocols outside the approved OT baseline (MITRE ATT&CK for ICS T0886), limiting lateral movement and unsafe cross-zone traffic.

## Implementation

Requires Splunk ES and OT Security Add-on. Maintain an allow-list lookup aligned with OT network design. Tune false positives per site. Route notables into ES incident review with MITRE T0886 tagging.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: OT Security Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 5151).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot` `sourcetype="ics:firewall"` fields `src`, `dest`, `dest_port`, `protocol`, `zone`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Requires Splunk ES and OT Security Add-on. Maintain an allow-list lookup aligned with OT network design. Tune false positives per site. Route notables into ES incident review with MITRE T0886 tagging.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="ics:firewall"
| eval proto=upper(coalesce(protocol, app_proto))
| lookup ot_protocol_allowlist proto OUTPUT allowed
| where isnull(allowed) OR allowed=0
| stats count earliest(_time) as first_seen latest(_time) as last_seen values(dest) as dest_ips by src, proto, dest_port, zone
| where count>0
```

Understanding this SPL

**ICS Protocol Allow-List Violation** ‚Äî Detects protocols outside the approved OT baseline (MITRE ATT&CK for ICS T0886), limiting lateral movement and unsafe cross-zone traffic.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot` `sourcetype="ics:firewall"` fields `src`, `dest`, `dest_port`, `protocol`, `zone`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): OT Security Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 5151). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: ics:firewall. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="ics:firewall". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **proto** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where isnull(allowed) OR allowed=0` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src, proto, dest_port, zone** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where count>0` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare results to the **OT Security** add-on views, the **OT asset** inventory, and any **engineering change** or CMMS record. When you send ot-data through **Nozomi**, match the same **Nozomi Guardian** alert. When you use **Cyber Vision** sourcetype `cisco:cybervision`, line up the same event in the **Cyber Vision** UI. For raw ICS **syslog**, match the sensor or PLC console for that minute.



Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Sankey (zone to protocol), Stacked bar by protocol, Table of violating endpoints.


## SPL

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="ics:firewall"
| eval proto=upper(coalesce(protocol, app_proto))
| lookup ot_protocol_allowlist proto OUTPUT allowed
| where isnull(allowed) OR allowed=0
| stats count earliest(_time) as first_seen latest(_time) as last_seen values(dest) as dest_ips by src, proto, dest_port, zone
| where count>0
```
